byMatthew Jacobson, KUTV

SALT LAKE CITY (KUTV) —In an announcement the entire world could see coming, the International Olympic Committee has made its decision:Salt Lake City will hostthe 2034 Winter Olympic Games.

The foregone conclusion, with no other cities bidding for the 2034 spot, was made formal at the IOC's 142nd session in Paris as the city celebrated the opening of the 2024 Summer Games.

The committee had conditionally awarded the 2030 Winter Olympicsto the French Alpsearlier in its meeting, though its finalization will be made laterdue to current parliamentary issuesin France.



The 107-member governing body of the biennial, biseasonal worldwide tournament conducted its vote after discussion and statements from individual members - mostly centering on anti-doping efforts in the wake of revelations that nearly two dozen swimmers had tested positive for a banned substance before the Tokyo Games - and were stillallowed to compete without ramifications.

Many of the members commended Utah on its dedication to anti-performance-enhancing drug efforts during their comments.

Another theme among their comments was Utah's readiness to host a second event. Members of the International Olympic Committee came to Salt Lake City in April for an up-close inspection of its venues and infrastructure to determine if it was, as Utah Lt. Gov. Deidre Hendersonsaid at the time,  "more prepared for the Olympics" than any other city on the planet.





"No one wants them more," Henderson said during the IOC's Future Host Commission visit, which rang true in more ways than one due to Salt Lake City's status as the lone 2034 bidder.

The commissionrecommended a second OlympicGamesin Salt Lake City shortly after its visit.

At Wednesday's meeting, the IOC members who spoke gave glowing individual endorsements of Salt Lake City, and with previous recommendation from the commission and lack of competition,there was little, if any, doubt of a majority vote.

In a decade's time, world will be welcomed back to Utah, just over three decades since the state'sinaugural Olympic-hosting gig.A recent study from the Kem C. Gardner Institute found that some of Utah might not look familiar to those who remember the Winter Games at the dawn of the new millennium. One of the biggest changes since 2002is the population's diversity.

Salt Lake City's delegation in Paris was made up of leaders like Gov. Spencer Cox and Salt Lake City Mayor Erin Mendenhall, along with veteran and budding Utah Olympians, and the Utah group responsible for directing the efforts to bring the Winter Games back to the Beehive State.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: It's official: Salt Lake City will host 2034 Winter Olympics
description: Salt Lake City is going to host its second Winter Olympics in just 10 short years.
